title: Bobbys Acting Career

jokes.text: Do you mind the dog being in the cab, do you? / No. He doesn't mind me being in it, does he?
jokes.setup: Passenger politely asks if dog is okay in cab
jokes.punchline: Bobby flips the concern to the dog's perspective on him
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal with good economy - takes expected politeness and flips perspective cleverly

jokes.text: You can probably guess why I call him Hamlet. / Grew up in a small town? / No. Because he's a Great Dane.
jokes.setup: Passenger assumes obvious dog name origin
jokes.punchline: Literary reference - Hamlet the Dane
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection with literary payoff - assumes audience knows Shakespeare, well-structured

jokes.text: "O, what a rogue and peasant slave am I!"
jokes.setup: Dog named Hamlet should 'speak'
jokes.punchline: Bobby quotes Hamlet instead of the dog
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Bobby unconsciously becomes the Hamlet the dog won't be

jokes.text: That's okay. If he thinks of something to say later on he can call me.
jokes.setup: Dog fails to speak on command
jokes.punchline: Bobby treats dog like a person who might call back
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery treating absurd situation as normal - excellent economy

jokes.text: I said paw, not floor.
jokes.setup: Dog lies down instead of giving paw
jokes.punchline: Passenger corrects dog with pun on similar sounds
jokes.explanation: Simple pun, functional but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Don't hit the dog in my cab.
jokes.setup: Passenger starts hitting dog for not performing
jokes.punchline: Bobby's concern is about location, not the hitting itself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bobby's misplaced priorities - caring more about his cab than animal welfare

jokes.text: Let me go, Tony. I got to hit something. / Hey, what are you crazy? / I can see you're upset, but, uh, don't hit that. You could really hurt yourself.
jokes.setup: Bobby needs to hit something out of frustration
jokes.punchline: Tony's concern is for Bobby hurting himself, not the object
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Tony's protective nature misdirected in funny way

jokes.text: Hit me. Here, in the stomach. Go ahead. / I can't do that. / Come on, don't worry about it. I'm hard as a rock. I do 200 sit-ups a day.
jokes.setup: Tony offers himself as a target, boasts about his fitness
jokes.punchline: Setting up for inevitable contradiction
jokes.explanation: Sets up Tony's overconfidence for the payoff - good character consistency

jokes.text: Oh, Tony! I'm sorry. Are you okay? / I'm fine. I'm fine. / Hey, listen, if it makes you feel any better, I feel great.
jokes.setup: Tony's boast about being tough
jokes.punchline: Gets hurt but tries to maintain dignity while helping Bobby feel better
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to setup with great character work - Tony's selflessness even when hurt

jokes.text: Is it all right if John uses the toilet? / Just lay off, Louie, okay?
jokes.setup: Bobby is clearly stressed
jokes.punchline: Louie asks permission for basic human needs at worst possible time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - petty authority at inappropriate time, great character integration

jokes.text: Where did you come from, Bobby? / The Bronx.
jokes.setup: Tony asks about Bobby's origins (expecting distant place)
jokes.punchline: Bobby names nearby NYC borough
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation of expectations - treats local move like major life change

jokes.text: What dog? / Well, that dog. / That's my dog. / I don't see any dog. What dog are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Dog owner wants his dog back
jokes.punchline: Everyone pretends there's no dog present
jokes.explanation: Good group comedy moment - absurd denial of obvious reality

jokes.text: Do old ladies get mugged? / Well, that dog is my dog, and I want him now.
jokes.setup: Dog owner asks if Louie is in charge
jokes.punchline: Responds to authority question with completely unrelated question
jokes.explanation: Random non-sequitur that's amusing but not particularly well-crafted

jokes.text: Uh... yeah... I got something in my eyes. I can't see anything. Anybody got a dollar bill I could wipe the sweat out of my eyes?
jokes.setup: Louie asked if he sees a dog
jokes.punchline: Claims eye problems but asks for money, not tissue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - turns crisis into money-making opportunity with absurd excuse

jokes.text: Sure, you want somebody to talk to you? Yeah. Latka.
jokes.setup: Dog owner demands someone talk to him
jokes.punchline: Alex volunteers Latka, who speaks broken English
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection using character traits - sets up confused confrontation

jokes.text: I didn't get the part, Hamlet.
jokes.setup: Bobby's big career moment
jokes.punchline: Confides devastating news to a dog
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny - shows Bobby's isolation and the dog as his only confidant

jokes.text: I'll let you have him, but only 'cause your dreams are crashing around you.
jokes.setup: Louie shares the dog
jokes.punchline: Cites Bobby's failure as the reason for generosity
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - finds cruel honesty in moment of apparent kindness, perfectly in character

jokes.text: If you want to find a good job acting, find a line to stand in.
jokes.setup: Louie gives career advice
jokes.punchline: Treats acting like unemployment line
jokes.explanation: Good Louie logic - reduces artistic pursuit to bureaucratic process

jokes.text: "My broker is EF Hutton, and EF Hutton says..."
jokes.setup: Commercial audition for famous 'when EF Hutton talks, people listen' ad
jokes.punchline: Everyone turns around and listens (the commercial's gimmick)
jokes.explanation: Period-specific commercial reference - funny if you know the ad, but relies on external knowledge

jokes.text: You, uh, think I got a shot? / Yeah, you got a shot!
jokes.setup: Bobby asks about his chances
jokes.punchline: Tom's overly enthusiastic response suggests the opposite
jokes.explanation: Standard 'false hope' moment - tone suggests Bobby has no chance

jokes.text: Did you get the commercial? / Just wait a minute. / Are you going to tell us or not? / Well, wait till everybody's inside. / Did you get it? / I didn't get it! / What? / So what are we celebrating, then?
jokes.setup: Bobby seems excited, builds suspense
jokes.punchline: Reveals failure but they're still celebrating
jokes.explanation: Good build-up and misdirection - confusion about celebrating failure

jokes.text: You got your first acting job? / Not yet! / Yay! Yay! Yay! / What?
jokes.setup: Continued confusion about what they're celebrating
jokes.punchline: Celebrating something that hasn't happened yet
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic - celebrating future possibility creates confusion and humor

jokes.text: No, no, no, no. / Latka, if you don't mind, I'll make the toast.
jokes.setup: Latka attempts to make toast in his broken English
jokes.punchline: Bobby politely takes over after hearing Latka struggle
jokes.explanation: Character-driven moment showing language barrier humor and Bobby's politeness

jokes.text: Well, that's simple logic. You see, when you're good at something you end up doing it. Now, I'm not going to end up acting unless I get a phone call before midnight. So... I'm gonna get a call before midnight.
jokes.setup: Bobby explains why he'll definitely get the call
jokes.punchline: Circular reasoning that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect character delusion - Bobby's desperate logic sounds reasonable to him but is clearly flawed

jokes.text: So what if you're falling off the cliff? The view is wonderful on the way down.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bobby's false optimism
jokes.punchline: Louie's cynical metaphor about enjoying delusion before reality hits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor with perfect economy - captures the dark comedy of false hope beautifully

jokes.text: Maybe you're not that good. Yet.
jokes.setup: Alex trying to be gentle about Bobby's talent
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to soften harsh truth
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Alex's attempt at diplomacy makes it worse

jokes.text: it feels like, uh... like my father just died again.
jokes.setup: Bobby explaining how Alex hurt him
jokes.punchline: Compares Alex's honesty to his father's death
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective - Bobby's overwrought comparison reveals his melodramatic nature

jokes.text: You still say I'm not such a good actor?
jokes.setup: Bobby's emotional outburst at Alex
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was all performance to prove his acting ability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Bobby proves his acting ability by fooling everyone including the audience

jokes.text: You bastard.
jokes.setup: Alex realizes he was fooled by Bobby's performance
jokes.punchline: Simple, genuine reaction to being manipulated
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Alex's grudging respect mixed with annoyance

jokes.text: Now, baby, ring. Come on, ring. Blap. Blap.
jokes.setup: Bobby waiting for crucial phone call
jokes.punchline: Making sound effects to encourage the phone
jokes.explanation: Good character desperation moment - Bobby's childish superstition shows his anxiety

jokes.text: Maybe your watch is off. / No. / It has to be by my watch.
jokes.setup: Elaine suggests Bobby's deadline might be wrong
jokes.punchline: Bobby insists his arbitrary deadline is binding
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Bobby's self-imposed rules must be followed exactly

jokes.text: Ten, nine, eight, seven, six, five, four, three, two, one, zero. Zero.
jokes.setup: Countdown to Bobby's deadline
jokes.punchline: Emphasis on 'zero' - his dreams officially end
jokes.explanation: Effective dramatic tension - the repetition of 'zero' emphasizes finality

jokes.text: Aw, what the hell. I'll give myself another three years.
jokes.setup: Bobby's solemn promise to quit acting
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks his own rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - shows Bobby's self-deception and eternal optimism, great callback

jokes.text: Why don't you just stand them at a distance and let them call the dog? Whichever one he goes to gets the dog, right? / This guy ought to run for borough president.
jokes.setup: Officer suggests Solomon-like solution
jokes.punchline: Louie's sarcastic political comment
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Louie's cynicism about simple solutions

jokes.text: What was that all about? / I just want to make sure no one's carrying any meat.
jokes.setup: Officer does something unexplained
jokes.punchline: Checking for meat to ensure fair dog test
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff - unexpected practical concern in absurd situation

jokes.text: Hey, man, why would he do that? Go with the guy that beats him? / It's his dog! It's his dog! It's his dog!
jokes.setup: Dog chooses abusive owner
jokes.punchline: Everyone accepts this proves ownership despite the abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - dogs returning to abuse, people accepting flawed logic

tmdb_overview: Bobby looks for an acting job before his self-imposed deadline arrives.
insight: Bobby's acting obsession generates 36 jokes in 24 minutes—solid density carries a modest premise.
